dsl_rule.translation_notes: Classic moving average crossover rule. Direction is context-dependent: long signals on crosses above MA, short signals on crosses below MA. Exit conditions are reversal crossovers in the opposite direction, consistent with the stated rule. No confirmation bars required (crossover_confirmation_bars=0). Used sma_10 per the moving_average_period parameter.

backtest_rule.reason: Classic moving average crossover rule. Direction is context-dependent: long signals on crosses above MA, short signals on crosses below MA. Exit conditions are reversal crossovers in the opposite direction, consistent with the stated rule. No confirmation bars required (crossover_confirmation_bars=0). Used sma_10 per the moving_average_period parameter.

source_rule_text: Generate trend-following signals by comparing price position relative to a moving average threshold; a single threshold creates mutually exclusive, non-ambiguous signals.
